product_overview: Galawgaw is a web-based workout timer that runs a client-side, graph-capable runtime engine to execute block-structured workouts. The app focuses on accurate, delta-time based timing and an event-driven runtime so users can preview and control sessions in the browser.
core_goals: Run time-accurate, block-structured workouts locally in the browser
Preview the full execution plan (ordered steps) before starting a session
Control session playback: start, pause, resume, and advance steps without losing timing accuracy
Surface step-level events so UI and audio layers can react deterministically

features.name: Timer Engine Core
features.description: A client-side, delta-time engine (Zustand) that executes an immutable, ordered queue of steps and emits event-based state for UI consumption. Users can start, pause, resume, advance steps, and complete sessions with high timing accuracy.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Preview queue -> Start session -> Observe step:start event for the first step -> Observe step:tick events updating elapsed and remaining time -> Advance to next step -> Observe step:start for the next step -> Continue until session completes -> See session:complete event
Navigate to / -> Start session -> Pause session -> See session:paused event -> Resume session -> See session:resumed event -> Observe step:tick events continue from corrected elapsed time
Navigate to / -> Attempt to start session with no queued steps -> See empty state message indicating no steps to run

features.name: Smart Queue Generator & Preview
features.description: Converts block-structured workouts into an immutable, flat execution queue of steps (expanded repeats, injected rests, annotated metadata) and shows a preview so users can inspect the ordered steps before running a session.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Request queue preview -> See ordered list of steps with labels, types, and durations -> Start session from preview

Navigate to / -> Request queue preview containing conditional blocks -> See conditional steps marked as deferred evaluation -> Start session -> Observe conditional blocks evaluated at step execution time

features.name: Timer Engine Core
features.description: Zustand-based delta-time engine and smart queue generator
